sentences.daily: In my daily introduction, I usually mention that I am a punctual designer.
sentences.office: At work, people know me as a punctual designer.
sentences.interview: In the interview, I explained that I am a punctual designer.
sentences.business: For this role, being a punctual designer is a big advantage.
sentences.formal: I would like to mention that I am a punctual designer.
sentences.informal: Just so you know, I'm a punctual designer.

words.usageNote: Adjective + profession phrase — used after "I am a/an".

sentences.daily: In my daily introduction, I usually mention that I am a patient driver.
sentences.office: At work, people know me as a patient driver.
sentences.interview: In the interview, I explained that I am a patient driver.
sentences.business: For this role, being a patient driver is a big advantage.
sentences.formal: I would like to mention that I am a patient driver.
sentences.informal: Just so you know, I'm a patient driver.

sentences.office: At work, people know me as a humble waiter.
sentences.interview: In the interview, I explained that I am a humble waiter.
sentences.business: For this role, being a humble waiter is a big advantage.
sentences.formal: I would like to mention that I am a humble waiter.
sentences.informal: Just so you know, I'm a humble waiter.

sentences.informal: Just so you know, I'm an independent software tester.

sentences.office: At work, people know me as a calm accountant.
sentences.interview: In the interview, I explained that I am a calm accountant.
sentences.business: For this role, being a calm accountant is a big advantage.
sentences.formal: I would like to mention that I am a calm accountant.
sentences.informal: Just so you know, I'm a calm accountant.
